items.name: oracle-select-bind-nvl
items.sql: SELECT NVL(u.name, 'N/A') AS label FROM users u WHERE u.id = :id

items.name: oracle-q-quoted-string
items.sql: SELECT q'[Bob's order]' AS label FROM dual

items.name: oracle-national-q-quoted-string-unsupported
items.sql: SELECT nq'{Alice's order}' AS label FROM dual

items.name: oracle-minus-set-operator
items.sql: SELECT id FROM active_users MINUS SELECT id FROM archived_users

items.name: oracle-offset-fetch
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users ORDER BY id OFFSET 5 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY

items.name: oracle-rownum-filter
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users WHERE ROWNUM <= 10

items.name: oracle-join-bind
items.sql: SELECT u.id, u.name, o.order_no FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE o.status = :status

items.name: oracle-insert-values-bind
items.sql: INSERT INTO users (id, name) VALUES (:id, 'bob')

items.name: oracle-insert-values-multi-row
items.sql: INSERT INTO users (id, name) VALUES (1, 'bob'), (2, 'alice')

items.name: oracle-insert-select
items.sql: INSERT INTO archive_users (id, name) SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE status = :status

items.name: oracle-update-bind
items.sql: UPDATE users SET name = :name, status = 'active' WHERE id = :id

items.name: oracle-delete-conditional
items.sql: DELETE FROM users WHERE id = :id AND status = 'inactive'

items.name: oracle-repeated-bind
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users WHERE id = :id OR manager_id = :id

items.name: oracle-positional-binds
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users WHERE id = :1 AND status = :2

items.name: oracle-date-literal
items.sql: SELECT DATE '2024-01-01' AS created_on FROM dual

items.name: oracle-case-expression
items.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN status = 'A' THEN 'active' ELSE 'inactive' END AS status_name FROM users

items.name: oracle-exists-subquery
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users u WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id)

items.name: oracle-group-having
items.sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM users GROUP BY status HAVING COUNT(*) > 1

items.name: oracle-union-all
items.sql: SELECT id FROM active_users UNION ALL SELECT id FROM archived_users

items.name: oracle-intersect
items.sql: SELECT id FROM active_users INTERSECT SELECT id FROM archived_users

items.name: oracle-merge-basic
items.sql: MERGE INTO users u USING staging_users s ON (u.id = s.id) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET name = s.name WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (id, name) VALUES (s.id, s.name)

items.name: oracle-create-table
items.sql: CREATE TABLE users (id NUMBER(10), name VARCHAR2(64), created_at DATE)

items.name: oracle-create-sequence
items.sql: CREATE SEQUENCE user_seq START WITH 1 INCREMENT BY 1

items.name: oracle-create-view
items.sql: CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_users AS SELECT id, name FROM users

items.name: oracle-drop-table
items.sql: DROP TABLE users

items.name: oracle-truncate-table
items.sql: TRUNCATE TABLE users

items.name: oracle-transaction-control
items.sql: SAVEPOINT s1; ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT s1; COMMIT

items.name: oracle-grant-revoke
items.sql: GRANT SELECT ON users TO app_user; REVOKE SELECT ON users FROM app_user

items.name: oracle-comment-on-table
items.sql: COMMENT ON TABLE users IS 'user table'

items.name: oracle-for-update-nowait
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users WHERE id = :id FOR UPDATE NOWAIT

items.name: oracle-decode-sysdate
items.sql: SELECT DECODE(status, 'A', 'active', 'inactive') AS status_name, SYSDATE AS now_value FROM users

items.name: oracle-analytic-row-number
items.sql: SELECT id, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY status ORDER BY created_at) AS rn FROM users

items.name: oracle-timestamp-literal
items.sql: SELECT TIMESTAMP '2024-01-01 12:30:00' AS ts FROM dual

items.name: oracle-quoted-identifiers
items.sql: SELECT "Name" FROM "Users" WHERE "Id" = :id

items.name: oracle-alter-table-add-column
items.sql: ALTER TABLE users ADD age NUMBER(3)

items.name: oracle-create-index
items.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_users_name ON users (name)

items.name: oracle-drop-index
items.sql: DROP INDEX idx_users_name

items.name: oracle-in-list-binds
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users WHERE status IN (:status1, :status2)

items.name: oracle-delete-date-literal
items.sql: DELETE FROM users WHERE created_at < DATE '2020-01-01'

items.name: oracle-create-materialized-view-compatible-form
items.sql: CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW mv_users AS SELECT id, name FROM users

items.name: oracle-connect-by
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users START WITH manager_id IS NULL CONNECT BY PRIOR id = manager_id

items.name: oracle-legacy-outer-join
items.sql: SELECT u.id, o.id FROM users u, orders o WHERE u.id = o.user_id(+)

items.name: oracle-insert-all
items.sql: INSERT ALL INTO users (id) VALUES (1) INTO users (id) VALUES (2) SELECT 1 FROM dual

items.name: oracle-returning-into
items.sql: INSERT INTO users (id) VALUES (1) RETURNING id INTO :id

items.name: oracle-plsql-block
items.sql: BEGIN NULL; END;

items.name: oracle-create-procedure
items.sql: CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE p AS BEGIN NULL; END;

items.name: oracle-create-package
items.sql: CREATE OR REPLACE PACKAGE p AS PROCEDURE x; END p;

items.name: oracle-pivot
items.sql: SELECT * FROM sales PIVOT (SUM(amount) FOR quarter IN ('Q1' AS q1))

items.name: oracle-unpivot
items.sql: SELECT * FROM sales UNPIVOT (amount FOR quarter IN (q1, q2))

items.name: oracle-model-clause
items.sql: SELECT * FROM sales MODEL DIMENSION BY (id) MEASURES (amount) RULES (amount[1] = 1)

items.name: oracle-flashback-query
items.sql: SELECT * FROM users AS OF SCN 12345

items.name: oracle-match-recognize
items.sql: SELECT * FROM trades MATCH_RECOGNIZE (ORDER BY trade_time PATTERN (A) DEFINE A AS price > 0)

items.name: oracle-alter-session
items.sql: ALTER SESSION SET NLS_DATE_FORMAT = 'YYYY-MM-DD'

items.name: oracle-create-synonym
items.sql: CREATE SYNONYM u FOR users

items.name: oracle-database-link
items.sql: SELECT * FROM users@remote_db

items.name: oracle-explain-plan
items.sql: EXPLAIN PLAN FOR SELECT * FROM users

items.name: oracle-connect-by-root
items.sql: SELECT CONNECT_BY_ROOT name FROM users CONNECT BY PRIOR id = manager_id

items.name: oracle-insert-first
items.sql: INSERT FIRST WHEN 1 = 1 THEN INTO users (id) VALUES (1) SELECT 1 FROM dual
